rescue_claim_one_line: Systemic panobinostat failed in DIPG from inadequate intratumoral exposure, not target biology; CED-delivered aqueous MTX110 in H3 K27M+ patients bypasses the PK bottleneck and warrants rescue trial.

post_rejection_evidence.claim: PBTC-047 Phase 1 (n=53) of oral panobinostat in DIPG concluded myelosuppression-limited dosing with 'altered pharmacokinetics and limited exposure at the tumor site'; the trialists themselves attributed failure to PK, not pharmacodynamics — a mechanistic autopsy consistent with a delivery-route rescue.

PNOC015 (Wembacher-Schroeder et al., Neuro-Oncology 2023) demonstrated that repeated convection-enhanced delivery of MTX110 (aqueous panobinostat) in DIPG patients is feasible and safe, with imaging-confirmed tumor distribution and preliminary PFS signal exceeding historical RT-only controls.

Mueller et al. first-in-human single-catheter CED of MTX110 (NCT03566199) in DIPG showed no dose-limiting CED-related toxicity and quantitative distribution volumes (Vd/Vi) supporting therapeutic intratumoral panobinostat concentrations unreachable by oral dosing.

Preclinical H3 K27M DMG models show ONC201 + panobinostat combination produces synergistic cytotoxicity and cooperative restoration of H3K27me3, supporting the combinatorial arm proposed for the rescue trial.

mechanism_concordance.original_failure_diagnosis: Oral panobinostat in PBTC-047 was dose-capped by myelosuppression at 10-22 mg/m²; non-human primate data (Veringa et al.) showed brain:plasma ratios <5%, so CNS exposure at MTD was below the H3 K27M-sensitive IC50 established in Grasso 2015 HTS. Failure was PK/delivery, not target disengagement.
mechanism_concordance.post_rejection_mechanism: 2020-2026 CED studies (PNOC015, NCT03566199, NCT04264143) demonstrated that aqueous MTX110 achieves imaging-verified intratumoral distribution at concentrations orders of magnitude above the in vitro sensitive range, with no systemic myelosuppression ceiling. Independent preclinical work reaffirmed H3 K27M as an HDACi-sensitive lineage and identified ONC201/panobinostat synergy through converging restoration of H3K27me3 and ATF4/integrated stress response.
mechanism_concordance.stratifier_rationale: Grasso 2015 HTS identified panobinostat sensitivity specifically in H3 K27M DIPG cell lines; H3 WT midline and non-midline pediatric HGGs did not rank equivalently. Restricting CED MTX110 to H3 K27M+ newly-diagnosed post-RT patients both matches the preclinical biomarker and concentrates the expected effect in the tumors whose epigenomic state is most reversible by HDAC inhibition.

strongest_three_arguments: The original trialists explicitly attribute PBTC-047 failure to tumor-site PK — this is a textbook 'delivery rescue' where the rejected mechanism was never tested at effective concentration, and CED directly resolves the failure mode cited in the negative paper itself.

Three independent CED MTX110 clinical experiences (NCT03566199, NCT04264143, PNOC015) have already demonstrated feasibility, safety, and imaging-confirmed intratumoral distribution — the 'can it be delivered?' question is closed; only the 'does it shift PFS-12 in the biomarker-matched subset?' question remains.

The stratifier is non-speculative: H3 K27M is a binary, diagnostic, pathologist-adjudicable mutation that was the original in vitro sensitivity basis (Grasso 2015); the rescue stratifier is not a post hoc transcriptomic subgroup but the same biomarker the preclinical hit was selected against.

kill_or_confirm_proposal.experiment: Phase 2 single-arm CED MTX110 in H3 K27M+ newly-diagnosed DIPG post-RT, n=20 evaluable, with optional oral ONC201 combination arm; primary endpoint PFS-12 vs historical RT-only control (~20%); secondary endpoints: intratumoral Vd on gadolinium co-infusion MRI, H3K27me3 restoration on post-CED biopsy subset where ethically feasible, OS-12.
kill_or_confirm_proposal.go_criterion: PFS-12 >= 40% with bootstrap lower 95% CI bound > 25%; permutation null p < 0.01
kill_or_confirm_proposal.nogo_criterion: PFS-12 < 25% or lower CI bound <= 20% (i.e., indistinguishable from RT-only historical)
